content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of size NNN such that 1≤Ai≤K1 \le A_i \le K1≤Ai​≤K for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N. \\
Chef also has another array BBB of size MMM such that 1≤Bi≤K1 \le B_i \le K1≤Bi​≤K for all 1≤i≤M1 \le i \le M1≤i≤M

Chef can perform the following operation: \\
**1)** Select an integer XXX such that 1≤X≤K1 \le X \le K1≤X≤K \\
**2)** Append XXX to the end of exactly one array among AAA and BBB

Find the **minimum** number of operations required to make mean(A)mean(A)mean(A) **strictly** greater than mean(B)mean(B)mean(B) or determine it is not possible to do so.

For an array XXX of length MMM, mean(X)mean(X)mean(X) is defined as ΣXiM\frac{\Sigma X_i}{M}MΣXi​​. For example,


mean([5,6])=5+62=5.5mean([5, 6]) = \frac{5+6}{2} = 5.5mean([5,6])=25+6​=5.5
mean([10,13,20])=10+13+203=14.333...mean([10, 13, 20]) = \frac{10+13+20}{3} = 14.333...mean([10,13,20])=310+13+20​=14.333...
mean([3,3,3,3,3])=3mean([3, 3, 3, 3, 3]) = 3mean([3,3,3,3,3])=3

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK - the size of array AAA, the size of array BBB and the upper bound of values in arrays AAA and BBB.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1A_1A1​, A2A_2A2​, ..., ANA_NAN​ denoting the array AAA.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1B_1B1​, B2B_2B2​, ..., BMB_MBM​ denoting the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of operations required to make mean(A)>mean(B)mean(A) > mean(B)mean(A)>mean(B).


Output −1-1−1 if it is not possible to do so.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
Sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: A=[3,7,3,5,2,4]A = [3, 7, 3, 5, 2, 4]A=[3,7,3,5,2,4] and B=[8,3,5]B = [8, 3, 5]B=[8,3,5]. Chef can perform the following operations:

Append X=8X = 8X=8 to array AAA
Append X=2X = 2X=2 to array BBB

After that, mean(A)=3+7+3+5+2+4+87=4.5714...mean(A)=\frac{3+7+3+5+2+4+8}{7} = 4.5714...mean(A)=73+7+3+5+2+4+8​=4.5714... and mean(B)=8+3+5+24=4.5mean(B)=\frac{8+3+5+2}{4} = 4.5mean(B)=48+3+5+2​=4.5
Test case 2: There is no need to append new elements as mean(A)=4>2=mean(B)mean(A)=4 > 2=mean(B)mean(A)=4>2=mean(B).
Test case 3: As K=1K=1K=1, it is impossible to make mean(A)>mean(B)mean(A) > mean(B)mean(A)>mean(B) as mean(A)=mean(B)=1mean(A) = mean(B) = 1mean(A)=mean(B)=1 no matter how many operations Chef performs.
Test case 4: Chef can append 222, 111 and 222 to array BBB in three operations.
